summary: NEW FEATURES VERIFIED (backend + frontend E2E) for iteration 6: (1) MEDICAL DOCUMENT ATTACHMENTS per pet (upload to Emergent Object Storage, list, download, soft-delete, 15MB cap, isolation, cascade on pet delete) and (2) MULTI-PET CALENDAR (GET /api/calendar/events aggregates visits+vaccines+treatments across the user's pets, sorted by date, with per-user isolation; UI shows month grid + legend + 'Eventi del mese' list + prev/next nav). Backend: 71/71 pytest tests pass sequentially in ~73s (added 13 new TestDocuments + 4 new TestCalendar cases on top of the 54 from iteration_5). All new tests are green: (a) list-empty, (b) upload txt (referto), (c) list shows uploaded doc without storage_path leaking + no _id, (d) upload defaults category='altro' when omitted, (e) download returns exact bytes with content-type text/plain, (f) upload/list/download all require auth (401 anonymous), (g) cross-user isolation: admin cannot list/download another user's doc (404) and cannot upload to another user's pet (404), (h) files >15MB rejected 400 with Italian detail, (i) soft-delete: DELETE returns {ok:true}, doc disappears from list, subsequent download 404s, (j) delete_pet cascades — remaining docs become is_deleted=true and download 404s. Calendar tests confirm: empty list when no pets, all three event types included (visits by date, vaccines & treatments by next_due, entries with null next_due excluded), each event has exactly {date,type,title,pet_id,pet_name}, ascending sort globally, 401 anonymous, and strict per-user isolation (admin's /calendar/events does not contain another user's TEST_ events). FRONTEND E2E on public URL (Chromium 1440x900, admin login): (a) 'Calendario' nav link works, calendar-grid renders, cal-label shows Italian month name ('Luglio 2026'), cal-next/cal-prev navigate months correctly and toggle back to same label; (b) seeded a visit (date 2026-07-20) and a vaccine (next_due 2026-07-15) via API and observed 2 calendar-event badges on the correct days in the grid + 2 month-event-row cards in the 'Eventi del mese' list; (c) clicking a calendar-event navigates to /pet/{id}; (d) on PetDetail, tab-documents renders category select (Referto/Analisi/Ricetta/Libretto/Altro) + hidden file input + 'Carica documento' button + hint 'PDF o immagini, max 15MB'; (e) changing category to 'Analisi' and uploading pawcare_ui_test.txt produced a document-row with correct file name and 'Analisi · 11/07/2026' subtitle + toast 'Documento caricato'; (f) download button triggered a real GET /api/documents/{id}/download → HTTP 200 (blob opened in new tab); (g) delete button removed the row and empty state 'Nessun documento caricato' appeared. Regression sweep (from iteration_5): auth, pets CRUD (delete cascade also soft-deletes docs — verified), records CRUD, weights, guides, AI advice, AI chat + memory, AI chat streaming SSE (delta chunks + done terminator + memory), push subscribe/unsubscribe/status/test/check-reminders, scheduler + admin-only /push/run-batch, PWA manifest+sw — all still green. No console errors, no 4xx/5xx during UI runs (only benign Radix Dialog aria-describedby warnings from the pet-create dialog).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: get_object returns (bytes, content_type) from the storage service, but download_document uses record.content_type as the media_type and passes get_object's content_type only as a fallback. This is correct (DB is source of truth), but consider adding an explicit filename-safe Content-Disposition sanitization: current code uses record['name'] verbatim in the Content-Disposition header — a name containing a double-quote could break the header. Low-risk since uploads use user's browser filename, but a `filename.replace('"','')` would harden it.

server.py upload_document: `file.filename.split('.')[-1].lower()` returns the whole filename when there's no dot (falls into `else 'bin'`), but for files with multiple dots (e.g. `report.v2.pdf`) it still picks 'pdf' correctly. Edge case OK, just noting.

server.py delete_pet: hard-deletes visits/vaccines/treatments/weights but only soft-deletes documents. This is intentional per spec (so storage objects can be re-linked or audited later), but the inconsistency should be documented — otherwise users may wonder why documents behave differently. Also, if a pet is deleted and later a new pet with the same id is created (uuid collision, practically impossible), the old soft-deleted docs would resurrect in list_documents. Practically irrelevant.

server.py list_documents/download: rely on {is_deleted: false}; if the field is missing on a document (older records inserted without the default), the query would exclude them. Since all inserts explicitly set is_deleted=false this is fine, but a defensive `$ne: true` filter would be safer for schema evolution.

server.py init_storage: the `storage_key` is a module-level global. In multi-worker deployments each worker holds its own key which is fine for GET/PUT (server-side is stateless), but the init call happens lazily on first upload/download AND at startup. If startup init fails (network hiccup), subsequent requests will retry — good. Consider logging when storage_key is refreshed.

server.py calendar_events: iterates ALL visits/vaccines/treatments for the user's pets with a hard cap of 2000 each; for power users with many pets this could truncate. Consider paginating or streaming.

PetDetail.jsx uploadDoc: sets `Content-Type: multipart/form-data` header manually — axios normally strips this and lets the browser set the boundary automatically. Explicitly setting it works because axios overrides with FormData semantics anyway, but the line is redundant and could break in future axios versions. Not urgent.

PetDetail.jsx openDoc: creates an object URL and schedules revocation after 60s — if the user closes the tab immediately the URL is leaked briefly. Consider revoking on `window.blur` of the popup or on next navigation.

Calendar.jsx: uses Monday-first week grid — correct for Italian locale. However `new Date(year, month, d)` will jump to previous/next month if day is out of range — the guard `daysInMonth = new Date(year, month+1, 0).getDate()` handles this correctly, no issue.
server.py still uses @app.on_event (deprecated in FastAPI 0.109+) — flagged in iterations 4 & 5, unresolved. Migrate to lifespan.

server.py uses sync `requests` inside async endpoints (/auth/google/session, upload_document, download_document, init_storage) — blocks the event loop. For low RPS it's fine, but consider httpx.AsyncClient or offloading to a threadpool. Flagged in prior iterations.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (added TestDocuments with 13 tests + TestCalendar with 4 tests; total 71)
test_credentials: admin@pawcare.it / admin123 (verified working). All new test users are ephemeral test_doc_*@example.com / test_cal_*@example.com with password 'Pass1234!'.
seed_data_creation: TestDocuments registers a fresh test_doc_* user + TEST_DocPet, uploads a small text file, and cleans up via pet delete (cascade soft-delete verified). TestCalendar registers test_cal_* user + TEST_CalPet1/TEST_CalPet2 with TEST_-prefixed visit/vaccine/treatment records. UI test creates a temporary TEST_DocUIPet under admin@pawcare.it and deletes it at the end — no persistent data left in the admin account.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run backend suite serially: `cd /app/backend && python -m pytest tests/backend_test.py -n 0 -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml` (71/71 in ~73s). Documents endpoints: POST /api/pets/{id}/documents is multipart/form-data with file (UploadFile) + category (Form, default 'altro'); response strips storage_path. GET /api/pets/{id}/documents excludes is_deleted=true docs and never returns storage_path. GET /api/documents/{doc_id}/download requires the doc owner (user_id match) and returns bytes with the stored content_type + inline Content-Disposition. DELETE /api/pets/{id}/documents/{doc_id} is soft (sets is_deleted=true, no storage object removal). 15MB cap enforced AFTER reading — a huge upload consumes memory then rejects; if tightening is needed the check should move to a streaming validator. Calendar endpoint: GET /api/calendar/events returns [{date,type,title,pet_id,pet_name}] with type in {visit,vaccine,treatment}, sorted ascending by date; entries with null next_due are excluded for vaccines/treatments. Frontend Calendar page at /calendario uses Monday-first week + testids cal-prev/cal-next/cal-label/calendar-grid/calendar-event/month-event-row. Frontend PetDetail Documenti tab testids: tab-documents, document-category-select, document-file-input, upload-document-button, document-row, open-document-button, download-document-button, delete-document-button.
rca of the issue: N/A — both new features work end-to-end as specified. Documents: full CRUD + storage integration + auth + isolation + size limit + soft-delete + cascade all correct. Calendar: aggregation, sorting, null-filtering, per-user isolation and month grid rendering all correct. Only unresolved carryovers from previous iterations are the deprecated @app.on_event handlers and sync `requests` in async paths — both non-blocking to the current MVP.
